title: Preview Environments (GitHub + DigitalOcean)
description: Spin up a live preview environment for every pull request. Comment /start on a PR, get a running app in ~2 minutes using DigitalOcean Droplets.
requirements: SSH key added to both DigitalOcean and SuperPlane
A setup script at scripts/preview-setup.sh in your repo
agentInstructions: The user just installed the Preview Environments app. This canvas creates ephemeral preview environments for pull requests using GitHub and DigitalOcean. Setup usually involves: 1) Connecting their GitHub integration (needs Deployments permission), 2) Connecting their DigitalOcean integration, 3) Adding an SSH key as a secret, 4) Updating the repository name and setup script URL in the canvas nodes to match their project.
agentInitialMessage:
Welcome! This canvas is a full preview environment pipeline for **examplehq/example** - it spins up DigitalOcean droplets for pull requests, runs your setup script, posts preview URLs as PR comments, and tears everything down when PRs close or after the 24h TTL.

The canvas is structurally wired, but it still uses template defaults. Here's the full setup checklist:

---

**What needs to be done:**

**1. Connect GitHub** - [GitHub](integration:github)
Almost every trigger and action in this canvas uses GitHub. It needs the **Deployments** permission to create deployment statuses, in addition to standard PR and issue permissions.

**2. Connect DigitalOcean** - [DigitalOcean](integration:digitalocean)
Used to create and delete droplets on demand.

**3. Add your SSH private key as a secret**
The [Setup App](node:setup-app) node SSHs into new droplets using a secret named `preview-digital-ocean-ssh-key` with key `private_key`. Add the private key that matches the SSH key configured in DigitalOcean.

**4. Update the repo and setup script URL**
The template uses example placeholders. We'll need to update:
- GitHub repository fields from `examplehq/example` to your repo
- The repo/app values in [Save Environment](node:save-env) from `example-app` to your app name
- The setup script URL in [Setup App](node:setup-app) (for example, `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/examplehq/example/main/scripts/preview-setup.sh` to your repo's URL)
- The SSH key name in [Create Droplet](node:create-droplet), if your DigitalOcean SSH key uses a different name

---

Let's start at the top. Connect the integrations, then come back and tell me:

:::survey
What is your GitHub repository? (e.g. `my-org/my-repo`)
- [input]

What is the full URL to your setup script?
- [input]

Does the secret `preview-digital-ocean-ssh-key` already exist in your org, or do you need to create it?
- I need to create it
- It already exists
:::

Once you've shared those details, I'll update the canvas nodes and get the preview environment pipeline wired up.

title: Preview Environments (GitHub + AWS)
description: Spin up a live preview environment for every pull request. Comment /start on a PR, get a running app in ~2 minutes using AWS EC2 instances.
requirements: SSH key added to both AWS and SuperPlane
A setup script at scripts/preview-setup.sh in your repo
agentInstructions: The user just installed the Preview Environments app. This canvas creates ephemeral preview environments for pull requests using GitHub and AWS. Setup usually involves: 1) Connecting their GitHub integration (needs Deployments permission), 2) Connecting their AWS integration, 3) Adding an SSH key as a secret, 4) Updating the repository name and setup script URL in the canvas nodes to match their project.
agentInitialMessage:
Welcome! This canvas is a full preview environment pipeline for **examplehq/example** - it spins up AWS EC2 instances for pull requests, runs your setup script, posts preview URLs as PR comments, and tears everything down when PRs close or after the 24h TTL.

The canvas is structurally wired, but it still uses template defaults. Here's the full setup checklist:

---

**What needs to be done:**

**1. Connect GitHub** - [GitHub](integration:github)
Almost every trigger and action in this canvas uses GitHub. It needs the **Deployments** permission to create deployment statuses, in addition to standard PR and issue permissions.

**2. Connect AWS** - [AWS](integration:aws)
Used to create and terminate EC2 instances on demand.

**3. Add your SSH private key as a secret**
The [Setup App](node:setup-app) node SSHs into new instances using a secret named `preview-aws-ssh-key` with key `private-key`. Add the private key that matches the EC2 key pair configured in AWS.

**4. Update the repo, setup script URL, and EC2 settings**
The template uses example placeholders. We'll need to update:
- GitHub repository fields from `examplehq/example` to your repo
- The repo/app values in [Save Environment](node:save-env) from `example-app` to your app name
- The setup script URL in [Setup App](node:setup-app) (for example, `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/examplehq/example/main/scripts/preview-setup.sh` to your repo's URL)
- The EC2 settings in [Create Instance](node:create-droplet), including region, instance type, AMI, subnet, security group, and key pair if the defaults do not match your account

---

Let's start at the top. Connect the integrations, then come back and tell me:

:::survey
What is your GitHub repository? (e.g. `my-org/my-repo`)
- [input]

What is the full URL to your setup script?
- [input]

Does the secret `preview-aws-ssh-key` already exist in your org, or do you need to create it?
- I need to create it
- It already exists
:::

Once you've shared those details, I'll update the canvas nodes and get the preview environment pipeline wired up.
